settings.title: Settings
settings.master_note: Images placed in the Master Image Folder will be automatically sorted into catalog folders when running a duplicate scan.
settings.migrate_help:
Migrate your notes from the old AstroCatalogueViewer application to the new AstroCat format.

This allows old users to seamlessly transition their notes without losing any data.
Your original notes remain safe and you can switch back to AstroCatalogueViewer without issues.
settings.duplicate_scan_none: Duplicate scan complete. No duplicates found.
settings.duplicate_scan_found:
Duplicate scan complete. {count} duplicate group(s) found.

Move duplicate files to the archive folder?
settings.archive_folder_required_message: You have duplicate images of '{object_name}'. Choose an archive folder to safely store your duplicate images.
settings.duplicate_files_moved: Moved {count} duplicate file(s) to the archive folder.
settings.migrate_notes_confirm:
Migrate notes from your old AstroCatalogueViewer installation?

This will not modify your original notes, and you can switch back to AstroCatalogueViewer without any issues.
settings.migration_no_previous_message:
AstroCat could not find a previous AstroCatalogueViewer installation.

This could mean:
- AstroCatalogueViewer was never installed
- It has been uninstalled
- No notes were ever saved in it

If you have notes in the old app, please ensure it's still installed.
settings.migration_no_notes_message:
No notes were found in your old AstroCatalogueViewer installation.

There's nothing to migrate, but you can continue using AstroCat!
settings.migration_unexpected:
An unexpected error occurred during migration:

{error}
settings.migration_success: Your notes have been successfully migrated to AstroCat!
settings.location_click_to_set: Click on the map to set location
settings.locale_restart_needed_message: The interface language will apply fully the next time you launch AstroCat.
welcome.title: Welcome
welcome.heading: Welcome to AstroCat
welcome.body:
This app helps you browse deep-sky catalogs with your own imagery.

Based on AstroCatalogueViewer

Quick start

  • Open Settings to choose image folders for each catalog.
  • Set your observer location so visibility hints match your sky.
  • Migrate your notes from the old AstroCatalogueViewer.
  • Use the filters and search to find objects fast.
  • Click an object to view metadata and add notes.

What's new compared to AstroCatalogueViewer

  • New catalogs added: LBN, SH2, VdB, and more (thanks to denis2704)
  • Separate notes storage: Image notes are now stored in a dedicated file
  • Notes migration: You can migrate your notes from the old AstroCatalogueViewer

Image naming

Filenames should include the standard object ID:

  • Messier / NGC / IC / Caldwell : M31, NGC2088, IC5070, C14
  • Sharpless : Sh2-155, Sh2-101
  • Lynds Dark / Bright : LDN1630, LBN667
  • Barnard : B33, B150
  • van den Bergh : VdB139, VdB152
  • Planetary PNG : PNG59.0-13.9, PNG104.2-29.6

Missing images

Enable Wiki thumbnails in the toolbar to preview missing targets while you build your library.

Feedback

Please share suggestions and bug reports via the GitHub repo issues page.
about.title: About
about.description: AstroCat helps you organize deep-sky catalogs with your own imagery, track capture progress, and plan what to shoot next.
about.quick_start_list:
1. Open Settings to set catalog image folders.
2. Set your observer location for accurate visibility hints.
3. Use filters and search to find targets fast.
4. Open an object to view metadata and add notes.

archive.folder_inside_library_message:
The archive folder is inside a scanned image folder, so archived files may still appear.

Continue anyway?
